en:
Trefusis was considered the ideal servant by everyone who met him.
He was discreet, efficient and almost invisible at dinner parties.
Wine was poured, plates were cleared, and every guest was attended to without fuss.
Guests paid him many compliments, and his host relied on him completely.
Yet perfection made the host feel strangely uneasy.
A servant who never made a mistake seemed too good to be real.
Trefusis, however, continued to serve as if nothing could be improved.
In the end, the host wondered whether such efficiency was a gift or a burden.
cn:
特雷弗西斯在见过他的人眼中都是理想的仆人。
他谨慎、高效，宴会上几乎不显存在感。
酒斟好了，盘子撤了，每位客人都被照料得妥妥当当。
客人们赞不绝口，主人完全依赖他。
可完美反而让主人莫名不安。
从不犯错的仆人，好得不像真的。
特雷弗西斯却继续服务，仿佛无可改进。
最终主人想：这样的高效究竟是天赋还是负担。

en:
On New Year's Day I made several ambitious resolutions.
I was going to get up at six, give up smoking and take more exercise.
For three days I kept every promise I had made to myself.
On the fourth morning the alarm rang and I went back to sleep.
By the end of the week I had accepted a cigarette and eaten too much cake.
My willpower, which had seemed so strong, lasted only a few days.
By February I had abandoned most of my plans.
New Year resolutions, I concluded, are easier to make than to keep.
cn:
元旦那天我下了好几个雄心勃勃的决心。
我打算六点起、戒烟、多锻炼。
头三天我信守了对自己的每一个承诺。
第四天早上闹钟响了，我又睡了回去。
到周末我接了一支烟，还多吃了蛋糕。
看似强大的意志力只撑了几天。
到二月我已放弃大部分计划。
我得出结论：新年决心好下难守。

en:
People have always tried to predict the future.
Prophets wrote in vague language, and experts built complicated models.
Few of their forecasts have proved accurate for long.
By the time one prediction is tested, the world has often moved on.
By next decade, many of today's warnings will have been forgotten.
Coincidence is frequently mistaken for proof.
Even the cleverest expert can be proved wrong.
The only safe prediction is that the future will surprise us.
cn:
人们一直试图预测未来。
预言家用模糊的语言写作，专家建立复杂的模型。
很少有预言能长期被证明准确。
等一条预言被检验时，世界往往已变。
到下个十年，当今许多警告将被遗忘。
巧合常被误当作证据。
最聪明的专家也可能被证明错了。
唯一稳妥的预言是：未来会让我们吃惊。

en:
A salesman praised a jar of cosmetic cream to a housewife.
He said it contained rare minerals and a secret formula worth a fortune.
She was not impressed and sent the cream to a laboratory for analysis.
What the tests revealed was mostly ordinary mud.
Mud is mud, she said, whatever fancy label you put on the jar.
The salesman had used every trick he knew, but facts are stubborn.
What looked like luxury was still mud at heart.
Packaging, she concluded, cannot change the essence of a thing.
cn:
推销员向一位主妇吹嘘一罐面霜。
他说里面含稀有矿物和值钱的秘密配方。
她不为所动，把面霜送实验室化验。
化验显示所含主要是普通泥土。
她说：泥就是泥，罐子标签再花哨也没用。
推销员使尽招数，但事实顽固。
看似奢华，骨子里仍是泥。
她总结：包装改变不了事物的本质。

en:
A television channel cancelled a long and tedious programme in the public interest.
Officials said viewers would be better off without it.
The public, however, did not agree.
Letters and phone calls of protest arrived in a steady stream.
There being no support for the decision, the channel faced an outcry.
Within a week the broadcast was restored.
It seemed that what officials called the public interest was not what viewers wanted.
The affair showed how hard it is to decide what is truly in people's interests.
cn:
一家电视台以公众利益为由取消一档又长又闷的节目。
官员说观众没有它更好。
公众却不这么认为。
抗议信和电话络绎不绝。
决定得不到支持，电视台面临强烈抗议。
一周内节目恢复播出。
看来官员所说的公众利益并非观众所愿。
此事表明，要判定什么才真正符合人们的利益很难。

en:
A beekeeper opened a hive while a wasp hovered nearby.
The wasp flew in boldly and began to steal honey.
The bees hesitated, unsure whether to attack.
In the confusion the wasp escaped with its prize.
It is hard to say whether instinct or cleverness won the day.
Bees act largely by instinct, but the wasp seemed to know when to move.
Survival may depend on knowing what to do and when to do it.
Perhaps both instinct and cleverness have their place.
cn:
养蜂人打开蜂箱，一只黄蜂在旁盘旋。
黄蜂大胆飞入，开始偷蜜。
蜜蜂犹豫了，不知是否该进攻。
混乱中黄蜂带着战利品逃走。
很难说本能还是机灵占了上风。
蜜蜂主要靠本能，黄蜂却似乎知道何时行动。
生存或许取决于知所当为、知所当时。
也许本能与机灵各有其用。

en:
Radio telescopes have scanned the skies for signs of other civilizations.
A greeting from Earth was sent into the depths of the universe.
Scientists admit that a reply is remote, yet the search continues.
It is worth trying, they say, however slight the hope.
No intelligent signal has been detected so far.
It is possible that we are not alone, but proof is still lacking.
The work has already taught us a great deal about the cosmos.
From the earth, at least, greetings have been sent.
cn:
射电望远镜扫描天空，寻找其他文明的迹象。
来自地球的问候被送入宇宙深处。
科学家承认收到回复的机会渺茫，搜寻仍在继续。
他们说，希望再小也值得尝试。
迄今未探测到智慧信号。
宇宙中或许不只有我们，但尚无证据。
这项工作已教会我们许多关于宇宙的知识。
至少，来自地球的问候已经发出。

en:
The Thames is London's powerful neighbour.
In most years it flows quietly, but in bad seasons it can threaten the city.
People would watch the level and prepare sandbags every spring.
A single storm could flood basements and block roads.
In the worst years water would burst the embankment and pour into the streets.
Pumps ran all night, but the river could do great damage before it fell.
The city depends on the river, yet lives in fear of its floods.
Our neighbour, the river, gives and takes in equal measure.
cn:
泰晤士河是伦敦强大的邻居。
大多时候它静静流淌，坏年景却可能威胁全城。
每年春天人们会观察水位、准备沙袋。
一场暴雨就能淹地下室、堵道路。
最糟的年头水会冲垮堤岸、涌入街道。
抽水机整夜运转，河水退去前仍可能造成巨大破坏。
城市依赖这条河，也惧怕它的洪水。
我们的邻居——河流——有予有取。

en:
After forty years abroad, the old man returned to his homeland.
He had dreamed of this journey for decades.
What he found was not what he remembered.
The house where he used to live had been pulled down.
Friends he had known were gone, and even the dialect sounded strange.
There used to be a market on the corner; now there was a car park.
He walked the streets alone, recognized by almost no one.
He realized he had come back to a place that existed only in memory.
cn:
侨居四十年後，老人回到了故乡。
他梦想这次归来已数十年。
所见却与记忆不同。
他过去住的房子已拆除。
熟识的朋友不在了，连方言听起来都陌生。
拐角过去有集市，如今是停车场。
他独自走在街上，几乎无人认得。
他意识到，自己回到的是一个只活在记忆中的地方。

en:
Collecting can be a pleasant hobby, but it can also become an obsession.
I knew a man who filled every room with matchboxes, labels and old papers.
He could not discard a single item, however worthless it seemed.
Collecting, which had begun as fun, took over his life.
He became obsessed with finding one more piece for his piles.
Visitors had to squeeze through narrow gaps between towers of boxes.
He insisted that everything was valuable to him.
I wondered where a hobby ended and a problem began.
cn:
收藏可以是愉快的爱好，也可能变成痴迷。
我认识一个人，把每个房间塞满火柴盒、标签和旧报纸。
他一件也舍不得扔，不管看起来多没用。
起初有趣的收藏主宰了他的生活。
他痴迷于再往堆里添一件。
访客只能从箱堆间的窄缝挤过去。
他坚持一切对他都有价值。
我想知道爱好何时变成了问题。

en:
The prisoner on the ship dreamed of freedom night after night.
He had imagined escape so often that the dream felt almost real.
One night he dreamed he was running through trees towards home.
The vision was so vivid that he rolled off his bunk in excitement.
He woke with a broken arm, still inside his cell.
He had been dreaming of liberty when the fall shattered the illusion.
For a moment he had believed he was free; reality returned at once.
His dream of freedom ended as suddenly as it had begun.
cn:
船上囚犯一夜又一夜梦想自由。
他想象逃跑太多次，梦几乎像真的。
一晚他梦见穿过树林奔向家。
景象太生动，他激动得翻下铺位。
他摔断胳膊醒来，仍在牢中。
他正梦着自由时，一摔粉碎了幻觉。
有一瞬他以为自己自由了，现实立刻归来。
自由的梦来得突然，去得也突然。

en:
I arrived at the station with plenty of time and strolled along the platform.
I thought I was early, so I did not hurry to the gate.
Suddenly the departure signal rang and I ran for the train.
The train had already left before I reached the door.
I was too late to catch it, though I had been too early to wait properly.
The guard smiled and said the train had left one minute ahead of schedule.
I had thought I was early; in fact I was both too early and too late.
Missing it by seconds taught me a lesson in punctuality.
cn:
我到站时间充裕，便在站台散步。
我以为还早，便不急着去检票口。
突然发车铃响，我朝火车跑去。
我到门前时火车已经开了。
我太晚赶不上，却又太早没好好等候。
站务员笑着说火车比时刻表早开了一分钟。
我以为还早，其实既太早又太晚。
差几秒没赶上，给我上了一堂守时的课。
